summary: Book information: 'Essentials of Strength Training and Conditioning' by Baechle and Earle, published by Human Kinetics.
Publication details: The third edition of the book was published in 2008.
Book structure: 'Concepts and Applications of the Exercise Sciences' has 10 chapters covering various exercise science topics.
Book organization: The book is divided into three parts, focusing on anaerobic, aerobic, and applying exercise prescription principles.
Author affiliations: The book authors are affiliated with various universities and organizations.

This chunk provides acknowledgments and reprint permissions for figures and tables used in the book.
Two figures are reprinted from the book 'Scientific bases of human movement' by Gowitzke and Milner.
Figure 4.13, on force-velocity relationships in elbow flexors and extensors, is reprinted from a biomechanics publication.
Figure 4.16 is reprinted from the NSCA Journal, focusing on a movement-oriented approach to exercise prescription.
Table 5.3 is reprinted from a PhD dissertation on physiological responses to overtraining from high-intensity resistance exercise.
Figure 5.6 is reprinted from the book 'Designing resistance training programs' by Fleck and Kraemer.
Figure 6.1 is from the 8th edition of 'Textbook of medical physiology' by Guyton.
Figure 6.2 is reprinted from the 4th edition of 'Exercise physiology: Energy, nutrition, and human performance' by McArdle, Katch, and Katch.
Figure 6.3 is from the 5th edition of 'The physiological basis for exercise and sport' by Fox, Bowers, and Foss.
Figure 7.2 is reprinted from the journal 'Pediatric Exercise Science' on the topic of resistance training and youth.
Youth Resistance Training Guidelines, adapted from a position statement paper on youth resistance training.
Figure 8.1 is reprinted from the journal 'Research Quarterly for Exercise and Sport'.
Table 8.2 is adapted from a 1976 study on attentional and interpersonal style.
Figure 8.2 is reprinted from the NSCA Journal on understanding anxiety in sport performance.
Figure 8.3 is adapted from a journal article on effective behavioral coaching in sport psychology.
Figure 9.2 is reprinted from the book 'Physiological aspects of sport training and performance' by Hoffman.
Figure 9.3 is adapted from a study on bodybuilders' body composition and the effect of nandrolone decanoate.
Figure 9.4 is adapted from a study on the effects of creatine and beta-alanine supplementation on athletes.
Table 10.3 is adapted from the 'International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values'.
Figure 10.2 is reprinted from the 'Diagnostic and statistical manual of mental disorders'.
Figure 10.3 is reprinted from the 'Diagnostic and statistical manual of mental disorders'.
Table 11.1 is adapted from 'Exercise physiology: Energy, nutrition, and human performance'.
Figure 12.2 is from the 5th edition of 'The physiological basis for exercise and sport'.
Figure 12.3 is adapted from an article on the 300-yard shuttle run in the NSCA Journal.
Figure 12.7 is adapted from an article on the T-test in the NSCA Journal.
Figure 12.8 is adapted from a study on the reliability and validity of the T-test.
Table 12.1 is adapted from 'Norms for fitness, performance, and health'.
Table 12.2 is reprinted from 'Norms for fitness, performance, and health'.
Table 12.3 is reprinted from 'Norms for fitness, performance, and health'.
Table 12.5 is adapted from 'The physiological basis for exercise and sport'.
Table 12.6 is adapted from 'Norms for fitness, performance, and health' and 'Explosive power and strength'.
